name: Bhai Satninder Singh Bodal
body: The first time I heard Bhai Satninder Singh Bodal was in a video from a samagam in Alwar, Rajasthan. He was accompanying his cousin on the side harmonium and I remember thinking that he was very skilled. Fast forward a year or two and I found him on Facebook, and that started off our friendship. Coincidentally, I was making a trip to India in 2014 and decided to visit him in his village, Bodalan. He was a very gracious host and before long we started talking about classical music. His interest began at a young age listening to his uncles, Bhai Dilbagh and Gulbagh Ji. However, he only started learning seriously about 7 years prior to my visit. Being a part of the illustrious family of Bhai Dilbagh and Gulbagh Ji, singing was second nature to him and he picked it up very fast. It is apparent from his singing that he combines the styles of Patiala and Sham Chaurasi Gharanas. Bhai Dilbagh and Gulbagh Ji belong to the Patiala Gharana and the Sham Chaurasi influence comes from his idol, Ustad Salamat Ali Khan. He is currently under the guidance of Ustad B. S. Narang from Jalandhar. Satninder Singh Ji has won 1st place in numerous classical competitions in India, including the annual festival of Harivallabh allowing him to perform at the main stage the following year. He is certainly one of the rising stars in Hindustani classical  vocal and the torchbearer of his family’s tradition of Gurmat Sangeet.

Below are some recordings from his tour in UK. Some of these were posted on Soundcloud by DGN sounds. Here is a link to their Soundcloud page for more beautiful recordings https://soundcloud.com/dgnsounds

The following Shabads are from the Akali Kirtan Darbar held in UK. These were obtained from the Soundcloud channel ‘Narsingh Bornlion’. Please check out the page for more recordings https://soundcloud.com/narsinghbornlion

The following recordings are from UK as well and these feature Bhai Sudarshan Singh Ji on tabla. These recordings were obtained from the ‘Sikh Vision’ channel on Youtube. Please check out the channel by clicking on the following link https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCTl4bBTb2F_ApverWWWk2ug

The following recordings were contributed by Veer Amanbir Singh. His brother had recorded these using his hand phone so there may be a bit of background noise in these recordings. Nevertheless, we hope you enjoy these recordings from Ontario, Canada. Veer Amanbir Singh has his own Youtube channel called ‘Kirtan Nirmolak Heera’. Please check out his channel at the following link https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCNPn_8FpWrxYpumk8aEhNVQ

The following recordings were obtained from the Sabh 13 Gurmat Arts Facebook page. Please follow their Facebook page for more wonderful content https://www.facebook.com/SabhTeraGurmatArtsKIRTANSOCIETY/
